Title: Polyketide reductases in defense-related parasorboside biosynthesis in Gerbera hybrida share processing strategies with microbial polyketide synthase systems

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Early gerbera transcriptome/microarray coexpression data (Laitinen et al., 2005); qRT-PCR; TLC-based metabolite profiling; in vitro enzyme assays; radiometric enzyme reconstruction assay; substrate analog assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to shift flux from gerberin toward parasorboside in gerbera by overexpression; likely useful for engineering defense-related polyketide composition rather than total pathway output; stacking with upstream G2PS1 and the unknown first PKR may improve pathway control; caution that altering product ratio may affect defense chemistry and co-suppression occurred in one OE line.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced linear triketide intermediate consistent with 3,5-dihydroxyhexanoic-CoA/parasorboside aglycone precursor
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear change in total gerberin+parasorboside pool was reported; primarily alters branch ratio. Possible unintended co-regulation/cross-suppression with GRED2 in transgenics.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of the proximal keto group of the linear triketide CoA-bound intermediate after the first reduction and before lactonization, directing the pathway toward parasorboside.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in in vitro leaves, carpel, and ovary; somewhat lower in anthers and roots; expression profile highly similar to G2PS1; lower in young and mature leaves than in vitro leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRED1 was identified as an SDR reductase coexpressed with G2PS1. 2. Overexpression of GRED1 increased parasorboside and decreased gerberin in gerbera leaves. 3. Double downregulation of GRED1/GRED2 abolished parasorboside accumulation and caused gerberin to predominate. 4. In vitro assays showed GRED1 does not reduce TAL, gerberin, or gerberin aglycone, but with G2PS1 and NADPH it forms a new reduced product and also reduces the linear triketide analog methyl 3,5-dioxohexanoate. 5. Therefore GRED1 catalyzes the second reduction step before lactonization at the pathway branch to parasorboside.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Reduced linear triketide CoA thioester intermediate, proposed 5-hydroxy-3-oxohexanoic-CoA; substrate analog methyl 3,5-dioxohexanoate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes parasorboside accumulation at the expense of gerberin.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in in vitro leaves, carpel, and ovary; somewhat lower in anthers and roots.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Gerbera tissues broadly, especially leaves and multiple floral organs; parasorboside enriched in in vitro and young leaves, receptacle, root, scape.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: parasorboside branch of the defense-related gerbera polyketide pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate precursor that commits flux toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic gerbera
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point control step; second reduction step determining parasorboside vs gerberin ratio
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related secondary metabolite implicated in environmental adaptation; aglycones accumulate under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / downstream tailoring step before lactonization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GRED1 and GRED2 are recruited TKPR2 orthologues from the sporopollenin pathway. Neither TAL nor gerberin/gerberin aglycone is the substrate, revising the earlier pathway model. Both genes retain some pollen wall function from ancestral pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: G2PS1 (functional pathway coupling inferred from reconstruction assay)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; transgenic overexpression; RNAi downregulation; qRT-PCR; TLC metabolite profiling; in vitro enzyme assays with recombinant protein; radiometric coupled assay with G2PS1; NADPH consumption assay with methyl 3,5-dioxohexanoate

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome similarity search; qRT-PCR; TLC-based metabolite profiling; RNAi transgenics; in vitro enzyme assays; radiometric enzyme reconstruction assay; substrate analog assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering increased parasorboside proportion; because GRED1 and GRED2 are functionally redundant, manipulating either singly may have partial effects whereas simultaneous targeting provides stronger control; caution due to cross-silencing caused by high sequence identity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced linear triketide intermediate consistent with 3,5-dihydroxyhexanoic-CoA/parasorboside aglycone precursor
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mainly changes gerberin/parasorboside ratio rather than total amount; high sequence similarity with GRED1 complicates precise editing due to cross-downregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of the proximal keto group of the linear triketide CoA-bound intermediate before lactonization, thereby favoring parasorboside formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in receptacle, scape, and in vitro leaves; lower in young and mature leaves; not anther specific.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRED2 is a GRED1-like SDR identified from transcriptome data. 2. Its expression correlates with parasorboside-enriched tissues/cultivars. 3. Severe double downregulation with GRED1 caused near-complete loss of parasorboside and accumulation of gerberin. 4. In vitro GRED2, like GRED1, failed to reduce TAL, gerberin, or gerberin aglycone but formed a new reduced product in the coupled assay with G2PS1 and reduced methyl 3,5-dioxohexanoate. 5. Therefore GRED2 functions redundantly with GRED1 as the second PKR in parasorboside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Reduced linear triketide CoA thioester intermediate, proposed 5-hydroxy-3-oxohexanoic-CoA; substrate analog methyl 3,5-dioxohexanoate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes parasorboside accumulation and is required redundantly with GRED1 for flux toward parasorboside.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in receptacle, scape, and in vitro leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Gerbera tissues broadly; parasorboside enriched in in vitro and young leaves, receptacle, root, scape.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: parasorboside branch of the defense-related gerbera polyketide pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate precursor that commits flux toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi construct in pK7GWIWG2D(II); attempted overexpression construct not successfully recovered as correct lines
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-point control step; functionally redundant second reduction step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related secondary metabolite implicated in environmental adaptation; aglycones accumulate under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / downstream tailoring step before lactonization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GRED2 shares 85% nucleotide identity with GRED1 and produced the same TLC-migrating reduced product with similar efficiency. Its ancestral relationship is to Arabidopsis TKPR2 from sporopollenin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: G2PS1 (functional pathway coupling inferred from reconstruction assay)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis across tissues/cultivars; RNAi downregulation; qRT-PCR; TLC metabolite profiling; in vitro enzyme assays with recombinant protein; radiometric coupled assay with G2PS1; NADPH consumption assay with methyl 3,5-dioxohexanoate

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR tissue expression; coupled radiometric in vitro enzyme assay with GRED1/GRED2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream committed-step gene for overall pathway activation; useful in stacking with downstream tailoring enzymes to control both total flux and product ratio; alone may not define gerberin vs parasorboside partitioning.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Linear triketide CoA intermediate and TAL as lactone/derailment product in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Initiates gerberin/parasorboside biosynthesis as the committed type III PKS using acetoacetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA to generate the triketide precursor; TAL is produced in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed; highest in in vitro leaf, carpel, and ovary; low in anthers and roots; similar to GRED1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G2PS1 is the previously identified committed PKS initiating gerberin and parasorboside biosynthesis. 2. Its expression pattern parallels GRED1 and broadly matches metabolite distribution. 3. In coupled radiometric assays, G2PS1 produces TAL and the precursor that can be further reduced by GRED1 or GRED2. 4. The study revises the pathway to place G2PS1 upstream of a linear triketide intermediate rather than TAL as a true in vivo intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to initiate biosynthesis of both gerberin and parasorboside.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed, highest in in vitro leaf, carpel, and ovary.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: shared upstream module for gerberin and parasorboside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related secondary metabolites important for environmental adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a primary experimentally used pathway gene in the Results and essential to interpret GRED1/2 function; however, the main novelty of the paper concerns GRED1/GRED2.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GRED1; GRED2 (functional sequential relationship in vitro)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior identification cited; qRT-PCR expression comparison; recombinant protein assay; radiometric coupled enzyme assay
